summary: Set 033 Session 4 of 6: shipped Layer-3 Playwright coverage of the H3 + H4 check-out contract that S1+S2+S3 wired. Two new spec files under tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/playwright/: (a) checkout-conflict.spec.ts with three passing scenarios — different-holder start_session refuses with EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT=4 and stderr names the holder's engine+provider AND both release paths ('--force' literal + 'Release Check-Out' literal); --force overrides and appends one audit line to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log naming both holders + session number + ISO timestamp (HOME-redirect via the new attemptStartSession helper's homeOverride opt makes the assertion hermetic); same-holder re-attach preserves checkedOutAt and bumps lastActivityAt (H4 same-identity branch); plus one test.skip on Release Check-Out command palette discoverability (Playwright palette filtering returned 'no matching results' despite the command being correctly declared and the view activated — the harness's existing note about palette/quickpick brittleness applies). (b) multi-in-progress.spec.ts with one passing scenario — 'In Progress (2)' bucket header assertion proves the multi-in-progress reader+renderer paint the live count parenthetical — plus one test.skip on per-row gauge SVG painting. Helper extension: added attemptStartSession(handle, sessionNumber, identity, opts) to electronLaunch.ts — subprocess-based driver that invokes `python -m ai_router.start_session` with an explicit (engine, provider, model, effort) identity and captures exit + stdout + stderr without raising on non-zero, optional homeOverride redirects ~/.dabbler/* via HOME + USERPROFILE env vars. Mid-session DISCOVERY: the gauge-SVG skip caught a PRE-EXISTING S2 reader bug — rows ship data-state='in-progress' but the webview receives accordionHtml === null in the snapshot payload, so client.js renders the chevron-spacer (not the chevron) and the accordion body never enters the DOM. Confirmed pre-existing rather than S4-introduced by re-running the existing session-sets-tree.spec.ts 'two in-progress sets each render their own accordion body' test against the same harness — it ALSO fails with the same shape today. The Layer-3 coverage S4 was tasked with adding caught a regression in S2's implementation of H2 multi-in-progress rendering that prior coverage missed. Skipped both the new and the existing tests with FIXMEs; out of S4's spec scope; deferred to a future S2 follow-up. Mid-session ARCHITECTURAL surfacing: operator raised three connected concerns — (1) AI engine wrote cancellation manifest as `_cancelled.md` while explorer expects `CANCELLED.md` (naming inconsistency); (2) explorer now expects CANCELLED.md but AI engine doesn't implement it (implementation gap); (3) deeper question — given S2's H2 verdict that session-state.json is the single source of truth, should CANCELLED.md / RESTORED.md / SUPERSEDED.md exist at all? Operator chose audit-then-spec response: Set 034 = audit of 'session-state.json as sole truth' (bundles naming-glossary scrub + marker-file retirement question); Set 035 = implementation. Saved as project_034_035_state_file_sole_truth_audit memory + indexed in MEMORY.md. The audit picks up the unfinished half of H2 — S2 retired the orchestrator marker but stopped short of retiring the cancellation/restoration/supersession markers. fileSystem.ts:276 still gates cancellation on file presence (`if (isCancelled(dir)) { state = 'cancelled'; }`), not on `status === 'cancelled'` in the state file. Verification: operator opted to skip routed Round A verification ('Continue without the router for now.') — the 4 passing scenarios assert subprocess-driven start_session calls + Electron-rendered DOM, both observable end-to-end; the 2 skipped have explicit FIXMEs naming the root cause. No router cost this session. npx tsc --noEmit clean on the extension. `npx playwright test multi-in-progress.spec.ts checkout-conflict.spec.ts`: 4 passed, 2 skipped, 26.4s total runtime. Cumulative routed spend Set 033 ≈$0.13 of $1.25 NTE (unchanged — no S4 spend).

reason.specifics: Session 5 is the queueing/polling feature — when a second orchestrator detects a held check-out from S1's refusal path, the extension offers poll/abort/force-override via a non-blocking UI flow. This includes structured detection-event emission from the Claude SessionStart hook + Codex configWatcher, a new CheckoutPollService.ts watching held set's session-state.json for transitions out of in-progress, the H4 identity predicate gating polling auto-retry, the dabbler.checkoutPollTimeoutMinutes setting, and Layer-2 + Layer-3 test coverage. High-effort holds: net-new state machine + cross-orchestrator coordination + new UI surface + new setting + tests across two layers. The S2 accordion-body bug surfaced in S4 is OUT of S5's scope (it's a reader-side bug; S5 ships new feature surface) and OUT of S6's scope (S6 is close_session cross-tier parity + docs + releases). Suggest tracking the bug as a parallel hotfix or rolling it into the Set 034/035 audit-then-spec follow-up — whichever the operator prefers when S5 starts.
